title: Theming
description: Using the SCSS structure below, you can customize your application’s theme or generate its output into CSS files, allowing you to create multiple themes for your application.

sections.title: Theme colors
sections.description: The $theme-colors variable is an array of colors, where each color can have a range — specifying a start color and an end color.It also supports different color formats such as oklch, rgb, and hex.The color names are reserved and include the following:

sections.title: Theme surfaces
sections.description: The $theme-surfaces variable is an array of colors, where each color can have a range — specifying a start color and an end color.It also supports different color formats such as oklch, rgb, and hex.The color names are reserved and include the following:

sections.title: Theme options
sections.description: The $options variable is an object that contains the options for the theme.The color-range option specifies whether a 50 to 950 color palette (as CSS variables) should be generated for each color defined in the $theme-colors variable.If set to false, you won’t be able to use the color ranges and will only have access to the surface variables for each color.The light and dark values are also fixed colors, used specifically for white and black.

sections.title: Full theme structure
sections.description: In the code below, you can see a complete example of a theme structure, which is included in the ACoreX style package.The different sections have already been explained separately, but we’ll go through each part for you below.
